topic: Glob tool implementation and prompts in major AI coding agent tools and libraries

sources.title: Claude Code Tools Reference (Official)
keyInsights: Glob tool is listed as a first-class built-in: "Finds files based on pattern matching", permission required: No
Grep and Glob are listed as separate tools, reinforcing the two-primitive design
Tool names are the exact strings used in permission rules / hook matchers / subagent tool lists
Docs do not publish the full Glob tool description string; it must be extracted from runtime

sources.title: Claude Agent SDK Overview (Official)
keyInsights: Glob listed in built-in tool table with phrasing: "Find files by pattern (`**/*.ts`, `src/**/*.py`)"
Example code shows `allowed_tools=["Read", "Glob", "Grep"]` as the canonical read-only agent configuration
SDK documents Glob + Grep as the paired discovery primitives for subagent exploration

sources.title: OpenCode glob.ts source (GitHub)
keyInsights: Exact parameter schemas including the verbatim 'DO NOT enter "undefined" or "null"' instruction for the optional path param
100-result cap, results sorted by mtime newest first, truncation notice emitted to model
Uses a `Ripgrep.files()` service under the hood with glob pattern passed directly
Empty-result message: "No files found" — short, single-line, no sibling suggestions
Throws when resolved path is a file not a directory

sources.title: OpenCode glob.txt description file (GitHub)
keyInsights: Exact description imported as DESCRIPTION constant: near-verbatim Claude Code wording
Mentions 'Use the Task tool for open-ended searches requiring multiple rounds' (OpenCode's analog of Claude Code's Agent-tool redirect)
Mentions 'multiple tools can be called simultaneously for batch searches' — permission model is concurrent-safe

sources.title: Gemini CLI glob.ts source (GitHub)
keyInsights: Most parameter-rich Glob schema: pattern, dir_path, case_sensitive, respect_git_ignore, respect_gemini_ignore
Structured error types: PATH_NOT_IN_WORKSPACE, GLOB_EXECUTION_ERROR — usable by the harness for auto-redirect
Sort logic: files modified within last 24h first (newest), then alphabetical for older — a twist on pure mtime-sort
Description references absolute-path bias but implementation accepts both
Error messages quote the pattern back: 'No files found matching pattern "{pattern}"'

sources.title: Gemini CLI file-system tools documentation
keyInsights: Display name is 'FindFiles' — interesting UX naming divergence from canonical 'glob'
Automatically excludes node_modules, .git without exposing the exclusion list
Output format template: 'Found {N} file(s) matching "{pattern}" within {path}, sorted by modification time (newest first):'
No manual approval required — auto-approved read-only operation

sources.title: Gemini CLI default-legacy.ts (tool definitions)
keyInsights: Verbatim glob description: 'Efficiently finds files matching specific glob patterns (e.g., `src/**/*.ts`, `**/*.md`), returning absolute paths sorted by modification time (newest first). Ideal for quickly locating files based on their name or path structure, especially in large codebases.'
Parameter description for pattern: 'The glob pattern to match against (e.g., '**/*.py', 'docs/*.md').'
Parameter description for dir_path: 'The absolute path to the directory to search within. If omitted, searches the root directory.'
Uses 'omitted' instead of 'optional' in parameter description — reduces undefined/null confusion

sources.title: Cline system-prompt tools directory (GitHub)
keyInsights: Cline does NOT have a dedicated Glob tool — ships list_files + search_files (regex with optional file_pattern glob filter)
list_files exact description: 'Request to list files and directories within the specified directory. If recursive is true...'
search_files has `file_pattern` param: 'Glob pattern to filter files (e.g., *.ts for TypeScript files). If not provided, it will search all files (*).'
Has three model-family variants per tool: GENERIC, NATIVE_GPT_5, NATIVE_NEXT_GEN — minor wording differences per variant
'Do not use this tool to confirm the existence of files you may have created' — explicit negative guidance

sources.title: Continue.dev globSearch.ts (GitHub)
keyInsights: Tool name: FileGlobSearch, display title 'Glob File Search'

Exact description: 'Search for files recursively in the project using glob patterns. Supports ** for recursive directory search. Will not show many build, cache, secrets dirs/files (can use ls tool instead). Output may be truncated; use targeted patterns'
Only harness in sample that pre-warns about truncation INSIDE the description
Only harness that explicitly says 'Will not show build/cache/secrets' — surfaces the exclusion behavior to the model
defaultToolPolicy: allowedWithoutPermission — read-only, instant, no confirmation
Single parameter: pattern (string, required). No path parameter at all — searches project-wide.

codeExamples.description: globSearchTool with readonly: true, isInstant: true

sources.title: MCP filesystem server (modelcontextprotocol/servers)
keyInsights: Tool name: search_files (NOT glob); description 'Recursively search for files and directories matching a pattern. The patterns should be glob-style patterns that match paths relative to the working directory.'
Parameters: path (required), pattern (required), excludePatterns (array, default [])
Backs pattern matching with minimatch — bash-compat semantics including `a/**/b` but NOT `a/**b`
Access control: restricted to allowed directories configured at server start
Also ships list_directory, directory_tree, list_directory_with_sizes — four separate file-discovery primitives
Directory tree returns JSON {name, type, children[]} — unique among harnesses for structured directory output

sources.title: OpenAI Codex CLI tool handlers (GitHub)
keyInsights: Codex CLI does NOT have a dedicated Glob tool — only list_dir (ListDirHandler), view_image, shell, shell_command, unified_exec
ListDirArgs: dir_path (required absolute), offset (1-indexed, default 1), limit (default 25), depth (default 2)
BFS traversal via VecDeque; appends / for dirs, @ for symlinks, ? for other types
File discovery happens through shell tool with rg --files -g; relies on model's RL training to use rg correctly
Sandboxing policy filters read operations before ListDir runs

codeExamples.description: ListDirHandler with paginated BFS directory traversal

sources.title: OpenAI Codex gpt_5_codex system prompt
keyInsights: Explicit guidance: 'When searching for text or files, prefer using `rg` or `rg --files` respectively because `rg` is much faster than alternatives like `grep`.'
No separate Glob tool documented — shell tool + rg is the full file discovery story
Fallback to grep or other alternatives when rg unavailable, but no specific instructions for find command
Same guidance appears in gpt-5.2-codex_prompt.md, confirming this is Codex's stable file-search story

sources.title: SWE-agent search tool config (YAML)
keyInsights: find_file signature: `find_file <file_name> [<dir>]`
Description: 'finds all files with the given name or pattern in dir. If dir is not provided, searches in the current directory'
file_name param doc: 'the name of the file or pattern to search for. supports shell-style wildcards (e.g. *.py)'
Does NOT support `**` globstar — limited to shell-level fnmatch
Also ships search_dir (search term in all files in dir) and search_file (search term in single file) — three separate primitives
Implemented via bin scripts (find(1) wrapper), not a native tool — so respects whatever find's behavior is on the host

codeExamples.description: find_file/search_dir/search_file config with signatures and arg descriptions

sources.title: OpenHands find_file/search_dir/search_file (Python)
keyInsights: find_file(file_name, dir_path='./') — docstring 'Finds all files with the given name in the specified directory.'
Signature identical to SWE-agent — both projects draw from the same Python-agent-skills lineage
No support for `**`; relies on Python fnmatch behavior which is single-level wildcard
search_dir and search_file are paired primitives for content-search at dir and file granularity
Note: the agentskills.py file marks this as a legacy V0 implementation deprecated for removal April 1 2026

sources.title: node-glob library (isaacs/node-glob)
keyInsights: `**` matches zero or more directories when ALONE in a path portion (so `a/**/b`, not `a/**b`)
`**` does NOT crawl symlinked directories unless {follow:true} is passed — silent source of non-determinism
Extglob: !(pattern), ?(pattern), +(pattern), *(pattern), @(pattern) — optional
Brace expansion: {a,b,c} and {1..3} — disabled via nobrace
Dotfiles: excluded unless pattern itself starts with . OR dot:true is set
Negation via `ignore` option, not `!` prefixes — differs from gitignore semantics
This is the engine behind Gemini CLI's Glob tool

sources.title: fast-glob library (mrmlnc/fast-glob)
keyInsights: Distinguishes static patterns (direct stat) from dynamic patterns (traversal) for performance
Follows symlinks in `**` by default; disable via followSymbolicLinks: false
Negative patterns: ['*.js', '!node_modules/**'] works inline, unlike node-glob
Extended globs: ?(pattern), !(pattern), *(pattern), +(pattern) available
POSIX character classes: [[:digit:]] etc.
Not used directly by any major harness but reflects the best-of-breed JS glob-engine design

sources.title: minimatch library (isaacs/minimatch)
keyInsights: Converts glob to RegExp; hence ReDoS susceptibility on adversarial patterns
`**` only special if it's the sole path component — `a/**b` does NOT match `a/xxxb`
Path separator `/` handled specially — 'foo*' does NOT match 'foo/bar' (diverges from fnmatch(3))
Brace expansion happens BEFORE other interpretation, so braces work even when invalid in bash
Engine used by MCP filesystem server's search_files
Docs explicitly warn: 'never use user-supplied input directly as glob patterns in production systems' due to ReDoS

sources.title: ripgrep user guide (BurntSushi/ripgrep)
keyInsights: `-g/--glob` flag filters files to those matching the glob; negation via `!` prefix
`--files` flag prints files rg would search WITHOUT searching; this is the file-discovery primitive
Glob patterns follow `.gitignore` conventions — later globs override earlier ones
Respects .gitignore, .ignore (takes precedence over .gitignore), .rgignore (highest precedence)
Also respects $GIT_DIR/info/exclude and core.excludesFile
This is the engine behind Claude Code Glob, OpenCode glob, Cline search_files file_pattern

codeExamples.description: rg clap -g '*.toml' and rg --files pattern usage

sources.title: Claude Agent SDK for Python
keyInsights: Claude Agent SDK gives Python/TypeScript apps access to the same built-in tools as Claude Code
Default toolset: 'Read, Write, Edit, Bash, and others' (per SDK docs)
Glob is usable via allowed_tools/allowedTools allowlist
SDK does not publish the exact Glob tool inputSchema — it's embedded in the CLI binary shipped as an optional dep
To inspect the Glob tool's actual schema from the SDK, one must intercept the CLI process or read from the running session

sources.title: LangChain community file management tools
keyInsights: FileSearchTool: name='file_search', description='Recursively search for files in a subdirectory that match the regex pattern'
Despite description saying 'regex pattern', the underlying implementation uses fnmatch (glob-style) — known misleading doc
FileManagementToolkit bundles: ReadFileTool, WriteFileTool, ListDirectoryTool, CopyFileTool, DeleteFileTool, FileSearchTool, MoveFileTool
BaseFileToolMixin enforces a root_dir (sandbox) — requests outside error with InvalidPathError
No mtime sort; no result cap; no gitignore awareness — shallow compared to Claude Code / Gemini CLI

sources.title: Cursor documentation
keyInsights: Cursor's agent tooling is largely private; docs do not publish the exact Glob/file-search tool schema
User-facing features reference codebase search, file search, and pattern matching but not the underlying tool names
Cursor is known (from community reverse engineering) to expose a file_search tool to the model but public documentation is sparse

sources.title: Roo Code (Cline fork)
keyInsights: Fork of Cline; inherits list_files + search_files pair (no dedicated Glob)
Directory src/core/prompts/tools has native-tools/ subfolder + filter-tools-for-mode.ts — suggests a mode-filtering layer on top of Cline's tool registry
Active development as of 2026 — one of the most-starred Cline-lineage forks
